commands.review_summary:
Adds a 30-second unref'ed timeout around sandbox media `scpFile` transfers so a hung `scp` child is killed and the staging promise rejects.

PR surface: Source +22. Total +22 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main only settles `scpFile` on child `error` or `exit`, so a spawned `scp` process that never exits leaves the Promise pending. I did not run a live hanging SCP setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs contributor proof and maintainer acceptance of the timeout policy; no narrow code defect was found for ClawSweeper to repair automatically.

target.title: fix: add timeout to scp spawn to prevent hanging in sandbox media staging
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the `scpFile()` function spawns an scp child process with no timeout. If the SCP connection hangs, the child process and Promise would remain alive indefinitely.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add a 30-second timeout (`SCP_TIMEOUT_MS`) that sends `SIGKILL` and rejects the Promise. A `settled` flag prevents double-resolution in race conditions. The timeout uses `unref()` so it does not keep the process alive.

## User Impact

Prevents hung SCP operations from leaking resources during sandbox media staging with remote hosts.

## Evidence

- **`src/auto-reply/reply.stage-sandbox-media.scp-remote-path.test.ts`: 5/5 passed** ✅
- Guard pattern follows `src/crestodian/probes.ts` local command probing with timeouts
